title: How to Set Up Auto Recharge in GoHighLevel — Never Run Out
description: Learn how to set up Auto Recharge and Smart Adjustment in GoHighLevel to keep your wallet funded automatically. Never let client campaigns stop again.
html_content:
Running an agency on GoHighLevel means juggling dozens of clients, their SMS campaigns, email sequences, and phone integrations. One mistake—letting your wallet balance drop too low—and suddenly everything stops. Clients panic. You scramble. Your reputation takes a hit. But it doesn't have to be this way.

Auto Recharge is the feature that keeps your GoHighLevel wallet topped up automatically, so your clients' campaigns never miss a beat. Whether you're managing 5 clients or 50, this smart system adjusts to your usage patterns and ensures continuous service delivery. In this guide, I'll walk you through exactly how to set it up, customize it for your agency's needs, and maintain complete control over your costs.

If you haven't experienced GoHighLevel yet, start your free 30-day trial here—double the standard trial period—so you can test Auto Recharge and all other platform features risk-free.

📋 In This Guide

  • What Is Auto Recharge and Smart Adjustment?
  • Why Auto Recharge Matters for Your Agency
  • How to Access Your Wallet Settings
  • Step-by-Step: Setting Up Auto Recharge
  • Understanding Smart Adjustment for Scaling Agencies
  • How to Customize and Fine-Tune Your Settings
  • Disabling Auto Recharge or Pausing Payments

What Is Auto Recharge and Smart Adjustment?

Let's start with the basics. Your GoHighLevel wallet is a prepaid account balance that powers SMS messages, email sends, phone calls, and other platform features. Every action—whether it's one text or one thousand—deducts from that balance.

Auto Recharge is an automated system that monitors your wallet balance. When it drops below a threshold you set, GoHighLevel automatically charges your connected payment method (typically Stripe or credit card) to refill your wallet back to a predetermined amount. No manual intervention. No service interruptions. Completely hands-off.

Smart Adjustment takes this one step further. As your agency grows and usage increases, Smart Adjustment automatically increases the recharge amount to match your spending patterns. If you were recharging $200 per month but now you're burning through $800 monthly due to client growth, Smart Adjustment scales up your recharge amounts automatically—keeping you ahead of your actual usage without overpaying.

💡 Pro Tip

Think of Auto Recharge like autopay for your utility bills. You set the trigger (when it recharges) and the amount, then forget about it. Smart Adjustment is like a dynamic utility budget that increases as your home's electricity demand grows—hands-free scaling.

Why Auto Recharge Matters for Your Agency

As an agency owner or manager, you're focused on results—not monitoring account balances. Yet a single oversight can cascade into serious problems:

Client Campaigns Stop Dead. Your client's SMS nurture sequence halts mid-flow because the wallet hit zero. The leads don't receive their follow-ups, and conversions tank.

Reputation Damage. Clients notice when their campaigns stop. Even if it's a 24-hour glitch, they're left wondering if you're reliable enough to scale with them.

Manual Scrambling. You're forced to manually recharge, manage multiple payment attempts, and explain delays to anxious clients.

Auto Recharge eliminates all of this. Your wallet stays funded. Your clients' campaigns run continuously. Your platform keeps delivering value without intervention.

How to Access Your Wallet Settings

Before you can enable Auto Recharge, you need to navigate to the right menu. Here's how:

1. Log in to your GoHighLevel account. Navigate to your dashboard using your credentials.

2. Click on your profile icon or name in the top-right corner of the dashboard.

3. Select "Settings" or "Account Settings" from the dropdown menu.

4. Look for "Wallet" or "Billing" in the left sidebar menu. This is where all your wallet and payment options live.

5. Click on "Wallet" to open your wallet dashboard. You'll see your current balance, recent transactions, and payment method information.

Once here, you're ready to configure Auto Recharge. Don't worry if the interface feels unfamiliar—the next section breaks down every step.

Step-by-Step: Setting Up Auto Recharge

Step 1: Confirm Your Payment Method

Before enabling Auto Recharge, you must have a valid payment method on file. In your Wallet section, look for "Payment Methods" or "Billing Card." If you don't see a card listed, click "Add Payment Method" and enter your credit card or Stripe details. GoHighLevel will verify it with a small charge (usually $0.01) that you'll see refunded.

Step 2: Locate the Auto Recharge Toggle

In your Wallet settings, scroll down until you find "Auto Recharge" or "Enable Auto Recharge." It will typically appear as a toggle switch, initially set to OFF.

Step 3: Set Your Recharge Threshold

This is the balance amount at which Auto Recharge triggers. For example, if you set it to $50, once your wallet drops to $50, an automatic recharge happens. Most agencies set this between $25 and $100 depending on their monthly spend. A safe rule: set it to roughly one week's usage.

Step 4: Choose Your Recharge Amount

This is how much GoHighLevel charges your payment method when the threshold is hit. If you spend $200 monthly, setting a $200 recharge amount makes sense. This refills your wallet enough to cover another month's activity. You can adjust this anytime.

Step 5: Enable Smart Adjustment (Optional but Recommended)

If available on your plan, you'll see a checkbox for "Smart Adjustment" or "Auto-adjust recharge amounts." Checking this allows GoHighLevel to intelligently increase your recharge amount as your usage grows. This prevents you from hitting the threshold too frequently and keeps your account ahead of demand.

Step 6: Turn On the Toggle

Once everything is configured, click the toggle to enable Auto Recharge. You should see a confirmation message like "Auto Recharge is now active."

Step 7: Save and Verify

Click "Save" or "Apply Settings." Your wallet will now automatically recharge based on your configured rules. Check back in 48 hours to confirm the system recognized your settings—you may see a test transaction or notification in your dashboard.

Understanding Smart Adjustment for Scaling Agencies

Smart Adjustment is a game-changer if you're growing your agency. Here's why it matters:

Imagine you start with five clients, each sending 100 SMS per month. Your wallet needs $50 monthly. You set Auto Recharge to $50. For three months, this works perfectly.

Then you onboard five more clients. Suddenly you're burning $150 monthly, but your recharge amount is still $50. Now your wallet hits the threshold twice per month instead of once. You get charged twice. And if usage keeps climbing, you'll be recharging weekly.

Smart Adjustment prevents this. It monitors your actual spending trends over 30–60 days and automatically adjusts your recharge amounts upward. So as your client base grows, your recharge amounts scale with it. You recharge less frequently, have better cash flow predictability, and avoid the friction of manual adjustments.

💡 Pro Tip

If you're just starting out, you can enable Smart Adjustment from day one. It won't make changes until GoHighLevel has enough data (usually 30 days), so there's no risk. But once you have historical usage, it kicks in and saves you from manual monitoring.

How to Customize and Fine-Tune Your Settings

Auto Recharge isn't set-and-forget forever. You should review your settings quarterly—especially as your agency scales. Here's how to fine-tune:

Adjust Threshold Amount: If you're recharging too frequently (more than twice monthly), increase the threshold. If you're worried about running low, decrease it.

Change Recharge Amount: After analyzing three months of data, you'll know your true monthly burn rate. Update the recharge amount to match this more accurately.

Review Smart Adjustment Activity: Some wallets show you a log of when Smart Adjustment modified your amounts. Review this quarterly to ensure the algorithm's adjustments align with your expectations.

Monitor Transaction History: Your Wallet dashboard shows every recharge. If you notice unusual patterns, you can drill down into client usage to identify high-volume campaigns.

Disabling Auto Recharge or Pausing Payments

What if you want to temporarily pause Auto Recharge—maybe during a slow period or while testing a new billing structure?

To Disable: Return to Wallet → Auto Recharge and toggle it OFF. This stops all automatic charging immediately. Your wallet will no longer refill automatically, so you'll need to manually recharge by going to Wallet → Add Funds and choosing a payment method.

To Pause Temporarily: You can also delete your payment method, which prevents any charges without fully disabling the feature. Just remember to re-add it when you're ready to resume.

To Completely Cancel: Disabling the toggle is sufficient. There's no subscription to cancel or contract to break. You maintain full control.

If you disable Auto Recharge, set a calendar reminder to check your wallet balance weekly. Forgetting about manual recharges is how wallet balance drops to zero happen.

The Bottom Line: Auto Recharge and Smart Adjustment are two of the most underrated features in GoHighLevel. They transform wallet management from a stressful, manual task into a reliable, background system. Set them up once, let them work, and focus your energy on what actually grows your agency—serving clients and winning new business.

If you're running multiple client campaigns and don't have Auto Recharge enabled, you're adding unnecessary operational risk. Your clients' campaigns—and your reputation—depend on continuous wallet funding. Set it up today, and sleep better knowing your platform will keep running without your constant supervision.
